title: New York State Route 15

infobox.value: [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390_(New_York) "Interstate 390 (New York)") / [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21") near [Wayland village](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York")

* [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20A in New York") in [Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(village),_New_York "Livonia (village), New York") * [US 20](/wiki/U.S._Route_20_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20 in New York") / [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") near [Avon village](/wiki/Avon_(village),_New_York "Avon (village), New York") * [NY 253](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_253 "New York State Route 253") in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") * [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390_(New_York) "Interstate 390 (New York)") in [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York")  [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") in [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York")

[Steuben](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York"), [Livingston](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), [Monroe](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York")

* [Interstate](/wiki/List_of_Interstate_Highways_in_New_York "List of Interstate Highways in New York") * [US](/wiki/List_of_U.S._Routes_in_New_York "List of U.S. Routes in New York") * [State](/wiki/List_of_state_routes_in_New_York "List of state routes in New York") * [Reference](/wiki/List_of_reference_routes_in_New_York "List of reference routes in New York") * [Parkways](/wiki/Parkways_in_New_York "Parkways in New York") | |

(**NY 15**) is a north–south [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") located in [western New York](/wiki/Western_New_York "Western New York") in the United States. The southern terminus of the route is officially at [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") (I-390) exit 3 south of the [village of Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York"), although some signage indicating that NY 15 continues south to the northern terminus of [U.S. Route 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") (US 15) in [Painted Post](/wiki/Painted_Post,_New_York "Painted Post, New York") still exists. The northern terminus of NY 15 is at an intersection with [NY 31](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31 "New York State Route 31") in downtown [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). Outside of [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), NY 15 is a rural two-lane highway. In the Rochester suburbs of [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") and [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), however, NY 15 is a major commercial strip, and the section in Rochester is a two-to-four lane street that serves commercial and residential areas.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

State highway in western New York, US

This article is about the current alignment of NY 15. For the former alignment of NY 15 in the Finger Lakes region, see [New York State Route 96](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_96 "New York State Route 96").

"NY 15" redirects here. The term may also refer to [New York's 15th congressional district](/wiki/New_York%27s_15th_congressional_district "New York's 15th congressional district").

| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | ←  [US 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") |  | →  [NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A") | | |

**New York State Route 15** (**NY 15**) is a north–south [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") located in [western New York](/wiki/Western_New_York "Western New York") in the United States. The southern terminus of the route is officially at [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") (I-390) exit 3 south of the [village of Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York"), although some signage indicating that NY 15 continues south to the northern terminus of [U.S. Route 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") (US 15) in [Painted Post](/wiki/Painted_Post,_New_York "Painted Post, New York") still exists. The northern terminus of NY 15 is at an intersection with [NY 31](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31 "New York State Route 31") in downtown [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). Outside of [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), NY 15 is a rural two-lane highway. In the Rochester suburbs of [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") and [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), however, NY 15 is a major commercial strip, and the section in Rochester is a two-to-four lane street that serves commercial and residential areas.

From [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York") north to the Rochester city limits, NY 15 is little more than an alternate route to I-390, which closely follows the route through northern [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") and southern Monroe County. South of Lakeville, the route is a regionally important highway that serves the villages of [Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(village),_New_York "Livonia (village), New York") and [Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York") and the [hamlets](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of [Conesus](/wiki/Conesus,_New_York "Conesus, New York") and [Springwater](/wiki/Springwater,_New_York "Springwater, New York"). It crosses [US 20](/wiki/U.S._Route_20_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20 in New York") and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") near [Avon](/wiki/Avon_(village),_New_York "Avon (village), New York") and overlaps with [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") between Livonia and Lakeville. [NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A"), an easterly alternate route of NY 15, parallels its parent from Springwater north to Rochester.

All of NY 15 was originally part of US 15. In 1974, US 15 was truncated to Painted Post and its continuation to Rochester was designated NY 15. For some time afterward, NY 15 was signed as a direct continuation of US 15; that is, it began in Painted Post and had [overlaps](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") with [NY 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17") and I-390 to Wayland. Officially, however, the route began overlapped with [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21") south of Wayland, and in 2009 the [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation") (NYSDOT) made plans to remove all signage for NY 15 on [I-86](/wiki/Interstate_86_(east) "Interstate 86 (east)") and I-390.

## Route description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Route description")]

Most of NY 15 is maintained by NYSDOT; however, the section of the route that lies within the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") is locally maintained.[[2]](#cite_note-inventory-2)

### Steuben and Livingston counties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Steuben and Livingston counties")]

NY 15 begins at [I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") exit 3 in the [town of Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(town),_New_York "Wayland (town), New York") in northwestern [Steuben County](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It heads north along an [overlap](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") with [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21"), connecting to [NY 415](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_415 "New York State Route 415") and passing the northwest end of the [B&H Rail Corporation](/wiki/B%26H_Rail_Corporation "B&H Rail Corporation")'s main line on its way into the [village of Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York"), where it becomes South Lackawanna Street. In the village center, NY 21 separates from NY 15, turning east at Naples Street at an intersection that also serves as the southern terminus of [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63"). The route continues north alone, becoming North Lackawanna Street for its last few blocks in the village before leaving it for [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"). Across the county line in the town of [Springwater](/wiki/Springwater,_New_York "Springwater, New York"), NY 15 proceeds through more open areas as it heads generally northward into a valley containing the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of Springwater, also officially known as Springwater Hamlet, built up around NY 15's junction with [NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A").[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/US_20A_and_NY_15_2.jpg/250px-US_20A_and_NY_15_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:US_20A_and_NY_15_2.jpg)

US 20A (here erroneously signed as NY 20A) and NY 15 just east of Lakeville

While NY 15A heads due north from Springwater toward [Hemlock Lake](/wiki/Hemlock_Lake "Hemlock Lake"), NY 15 leaves Springwater to the west, climbing out of the valley and snaking its way northwestward through the hills of the [Southern Tier](/wiki/Southern_Tier "Southern Tier") to the town of [Conesus](/wiki/Conesus,_New_York "Conesus, New York"). At the hamlet of Conesus, NY 15 connects to [County Route 71](/wiki/County_Route_71_(Livingston_County,_New_York) "County Route 71 (Livingston County, New York)") (CR 71),[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4) formerly designated as [NY 255](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_255 "New York State Route 255").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Past Conesus, the route continues northward across rolling, rural terrain to the [village of Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(village),_New_York "Livonia (village), New York"), located in the town of the [same name](/wiki/Livonia,_New_York "Livonia, New York"). NY 15 veers northeastward as Commercial Street for three blocks to Main Street, which carries the east–west [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") through the village. Here, NY 15 turns west onto Main Street, joining US 20A on a 2.5-mile (4.0 km) overlap along Main Street and Big Tree Road that takes both routes into the adjacent hamlet of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York"), located at the northern tip of [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake"). The two routes split in the center of the community, where US 20A continues west towards Buffalo while NY 15 turns north towards [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York").[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

The route heads away from Lakeville on a northwesterly alignment, paralleling Conesus Creek and to a lesser extent the [Livonia, Avon and Lakeville Railroad](/wiki/Livonia,_Avon_and_Lakeville_Railroad "Livonia, Avon and Lakeville Railroad") (LAL) to a junction with [NY 256](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_256 "New York State Route 256") on the Livonia–[Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(town),_New_York "Geneseo (town), New York") town line. NY 256 merges into NY 15 here, and NY 15 takes on its linear north-northeasterly routing as it heads into the adjacent [town of Avon](/wiki/Avon_(town),_New_York "Avon (town), New York"). Just north of the town line, NY 15 passes under the LAL and comes into contact with I-390 once again, this time at exit 9. Past the exit, I-390 turns to the north, following NY 15 as both routes cross 2 miles (3.2 km) of open, sparsely developed areas on their way to the hamlet of [East Avon](/wiki/East_Avon,_New_York "East Avon, New York"), centered on NY 15's junction with [US 20](/wiki/U.S._Route_20_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20 in New York") and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5"). Outside of East Avon, the two highways continue to follow parallel alignments for another 2 miles (3.2 km) to the [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York") line.[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

### Southern Monroe County

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Southern Monroe County")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/NY_15-253_in_Henrietta.jpg/250px-NY_15-253_in_Henrietta.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_15-253_in_Henrietta.jpg)

Southbound on the NY 15 / NY 253 overlap in Henrietta. NY 253 leaves NY 15 in the near foreground; off in the distance is West Henrietta.

Now in the town of [Rush](/wiki/Rush,_New_York "Rush, New York"), NY 15 takes on the name West Henrietta Road, which it keeps until the Rochester city line 12 miles (19 km) to the north. The route remains close to I-390 throughout most of southern Monroe County, crossing over the expressway and [Honeoye Creek](/wiki/Honeoye_Creek "Honeoye Creek") before connecting to I-390 at exit 11, an exit serving both NY 15 and [NY 251](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_251 "New York State Route 251"), which intersect a short distance east of the interchange. Not far from the exit is the [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") town line, at which point the route begins to pass through gradually more developed areas as it enters the outer suburbs of Rochester. In [West Henrietta](/wiki/West_Henrietta,_New_York "West Henrietta, New York"), the last hamlet of consequence along the highway, NY 15 crosses Erie Station Road,[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4) formerly part of [NY 253](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_253 "New York State Route 253").[[6]](#cite_note-west_henrietta_quad-6) Just north of the community is a junction with Thruway Park Drive, the current routing of NY 253 through the area.[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

Here, NY 15 takes part in its third and final concurrency as NY 253 joins NY 15 for one mile (1.6 km) across the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) to Lehigh Station Road, which NY 253 follows eastward toward [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York"). NY 15, meanwhile, continues on a northeasterly line toward Rochester, passing by the studios of local television stations, [WHAM](/wiki/WHAM-TV "WHAM-TV") and [WUHF](/wiki/WUHF "WUHF"), prior to entering a major commercial district surrounding [The Marketplace Mall](/wiki/The_Marketplace_Mall "The Marketplace Mall") and Jefferson Road ([NY 252](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_252 "New York State Route 252")). Within the district, NY 15 crosses a spur of the LAL and intersects NY 252 at a grade-separated interchange just south of the Henrietta–[Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York") town line. The route exits Henrietta for Brighton, but the commercial surroundings remain until Crittenden Road, a local east–west arterial linking NY 15 to both NY 15A and the [Monroe Community College](/wiki/Monroe_Community_College "Monroe Community College") campus on the latter route.[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

### Rochester

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Rochester")]

North of Crittenden Road, NY 15 passes through a pocket of residential development that borders I-390 and the adjacent [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal"). At the northern edge of the area, NY 15 meets and crosses I-390 for the final time at exit 16. While I-390 continues northwest from here, bypassing most of Rochester, NY 15 stays true to its linear northeasterly alignment, crossing the Erie Canal and entering the city's southernmost section. In this area, NY 15, now Mount Hope Avenue, serves the Monroe County offices and meets NY 15A at East Henrietta Road, completing the eastern alternate loop of NY 15. One long block to the north is Elmwood Avenue[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4) (formerly [NY 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47")[[7]](#cite_note-1977map-7)), which crosses NY 15 at the southeastern corner of [Mount Hope Cemetery](/wiki/Mount_Hope_Cemetery,_Rochester "Mount Hope Cemetery, Rochester").[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/North_end_of_NY_15.jpg/250px-North_end_of_NY_15.jpg)](/wiki/File:North_end_of_NY_15.jpg)

Approaching the northern terminus of NY 15 at NY 31 in downtown Rochester

The route continues on, running along the east side of the cemetery and the west side of [Highland Park](/wiki/Highland_Park,_Rochester,_New_York "Highland Park, Rochester, New York") as it crosses into a predominately residential section of Rochester known as the South Wedge. NY 15 finally breaks from the linear alignment it had followed since southern Henrietta and gradually gets closer to the nearby [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") as it heads northward through the neighborhood; however, it makes a sharp turn to the east just south of [downtown Rochester](/wiki/Downtown_Rochester "Downtown Rochester") to meet South Avenue in the shadow of [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") and the [Inner Loop](/wiki/Inner_Loop_(Rochester) "Inner Loop (Rochester)"). Mount Hope Avenue ends here while NY 15 continues east onto Byron Street, a one-block connector between South and South Clinton Avenues. At its east end, NY 15 turns north onto a unidirectional section of Clinton Avenue, passing over I-490 and the Inner Loop before reaching its northern terminus at the intersection of South Clinton Avenue and Woodbury Boulevard ([NY 31](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_31 "New York State Route 31")) in downtown Rochester.[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

Most of southeastern downtown Rochester's major streets are one-way, resulting in split routings for both NY 15 and NY 31 as they pass through downtown. Westbound NY 31 continues north from the north end of NY 15 on South Clinton Avenue to East Broad Street, onto which it turns west. Southbound NY 15, meanwhile, begins one block to the west at the intersection of South Avenue and Woodbury Boulevard (where eastbound NY 31 makes a left turn). NY 15 follows South Avenue, as well as a number of ramps traversing the Inner Loop and I-490, to the intersection of Mount Hope Avenue and Byron Street, where it joins northbound NY 15 on Mount Hope Avenue.[[4]](#cite_note-yahoo1-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/58/US_15_in_Rochester_NY.jpg/250px-US_15_in_Rochester_NY.jpg)](/wiki/File:US_15_in_Rochester_NY.jpg)

"To US 15" assembly on South Avenue in Rochester

The highway connecting [Lawrenceville, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Lawrenceville,_Pennsylvania "Lawrenceville, Pennsylvania"), to [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") by way of [Springwater](/wiki/Springwater,_New_York "Springwater, New York") and [Avon](/wiki/Avon_(village),_New_York "Avon (village), New York") was originally designated as [NY 4](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_4_(1924%E2%80%931927) "New York State Route 4 (1924–1927)") when the first set of posted routes in New York were assigned in 1924.[[8]](#cite_note-8) NY 4 was renumbered to [NY 2](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_2_(1927%E2%80%931939) "New York State Route 2 (1927–1939)") in 1927 to avoid numerical duplication with the new [US 4](/wiki/U.S._Route_4_in_New_York "U.S. Route 4 in New York") in the [Glens Falls](/wiki/Glens_Falls,_New_York "Glens Falls, New York") area.[[9]](#cite_note-9) At the [Pennsylvania state line](/wiki/New_York%E2%80%93Pennsylvania_border "New York–Pennsylvania border"), NY 2 connected to [US 111](/wiki/U.S._Route_111 "U.S. Route 111").[[10]](#cite_note-10) US 111 was replaced by an extended [US 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") c. 1938.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-1938map-12) US 15 was extended northward to Rochester by the following year, supplanting NY 2.[[13]](#cite_note-1939map-13)

Construction began in the 1960s on the portion of the [Southern Tier Expressway](/wiki/Southern_Tier_Expressway "Southern Tier Expressway") in [Steuben County](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York"). The section from [Painted Post](/wiki/Painted_Post,_New_York "Painted Post, New York") to [Campbell](/wiki/Campbell_(CDP),_New_York "Campbell (CDP), New York") opened in the mid-1960s as a realignment of US 15. The former surface routing of US 15 was redesignated as [NY 415](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_415 "New York State Route 415").[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) An extension of the expressway to [Savona](/wiki/Savona,_New_York "Savona, New York") was completed by 1971,[[16]](#cite_note-16) and the entirety of the highway through Steuben County was completed c. 1973. US 15 left the expressway at exit 38 in [Bath](/wiki/Bath_(village),_New_York "Bath (village), New York") to rejoin its original alignment.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-1973map-18)

In 1974, US 15 was truncated southward to Painted Post and the portion of its former routing north of Painted Post was redesignated as NY 15.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Around the same time, [NY 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17") was realigned between [Olean](/wiki/Olean,_New_York "Olean, New York") and [Corning](/wiki/Corning_(city),_New_York "Corning (city), New York") to use the Southern Tier Expressway instead of modern [NY 417](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_417 "New York State Route 417").[[18]](#cite_note-1973map-18)[[19]](#cite_note-1974map-19) The portion of the Genesee Expressway ([I-390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390")) from [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(village),_New_York "Avoca (village), New York") to Wayland opened to traffic in the mid-1970s.[[7]](#cite_note-1977map-7)[[19]](#cite_note-1974map-19) Although signage for NY 15 existed on NY 17 and I-390,[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-alps86-21) no such [overlap](/wiki/Overlap_(road) "Overlap (road)") existed in reality as the southern terminus of NY 15 was defined as I-390 in Wayland by the [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation") (NYSDOT) as early as 1977.[[22]](#cite_note-wayland_quad-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) In 2009, NYSDOT elected to remove the NY 15 signage along the Southern Tier Expressway and I-390, effectively moving the signed terminus to I-390 in Wayland as well.[[21]](#cite_note-alps86-21)[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## NY 15A

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_15&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: NY 15A")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/NY-15A.svg/120px-NY-15A.svg.png)](/wiki/File:NY-15A.svg)

[NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A") (35.19 miles or 56.63 kilometres) is an alternate route of NY 15 between [Springwater](/wiki/Springwater,_New_York "Springwater, New York") and [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") via [Lima](/wiki/Lima_(village),_New_York "Lima (village), New York").[[1]](#cite_note-2008tvr-1) It was originally designated as [NY 2A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_2A_(1930%E2%80%931939) "New York State Route 2A (1930–1939)") in the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)")[[25]](#cite_note-25) before becoming NY 15A in 1939.[[26]](#cite_note-1939changes-26)
